user_request: Draft a suit for damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. Reproduce the defamatory statements in the body of the plaint. Plead malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, and mental agony. Claim Rs.10,00,000/- as damages. Include prayer for permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court. Plead limitation correctly for defamation suits. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause.

facts.summary: The Plaintiff seeks to file a defamation suit against a Defendant for publishing false and malicious allegations on Facebook. The Plaintiff claims damages of Rs. 10,00,000 for loss of goodwill and mental agony, and seeks a permanent injunction against further publication.

slots.value: Facebook and other social media platforms

missing_fields: Date of publication (cause of action accrual)
assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with the District Court based on Plaintiff's residence or place of publication
Defendant's Facebook post is accessible to the public

R.10. Malice, Knowledge, etc.
Wherever it is material to allege malice, fraudulent intention, knowledge or other condition of the mind of any 
person, it shall be sufficient to allege the same as a fact without setting out the circumstances from which the same 
is to be inferred.
1. Condition of mind. Rules 10, 11 and 12 are no more than practical applications of the general principle laid 
down in r 2 above. Malice, fraudulent intention, knowledge, etc, constitute in some cases the material facts of a 
partys case: they must, therefore, be alleged in the partys pleading. However, the circumstances from which they 
are to be inferred need not be stated in the pleading.54
2. Malice. Malice is a necessary part of the cause of act ion in suits for slander of title and for malicious 
prosecution. It must, therefore, be alleged by the plaintiff in his plaint. Similarly, where a defendant in an action of 
libel claims privileges, alleging that the words complained of were used on a privileged occasion, the plaintiff may 
allege and prove malice, that is, improper motive.
3. Fraudulent Intention. For fraudulent intention, see notes to r 4, Fraud and coercion.
4. Knowledge. In a suit by a servant against his master for injury caused by the dangerous condition of a building 
where he is employed, the plaint must

A sues B in the court of second class subordinate judge for an account and values his suit for a court fee at Rs 
130. Under s 8 of the Suits Valuation Act, 1887, the value of the subject matter of the suit is Rs 130. The parties 
arrive at a compromise whereby the first defendant is to pay Rs 6,000 and the second defendant Rs 5,000 to 
the plaintiff. The court may pass a decree in terms of the compromise, although it has jurisdiction limited to suits 
of which the value of the subject matter does not exceed Rs 5,000.14

A sues B in the court of second class subordinate judge for an account and values his suit for a court fee at Rs

the plaintiff. The court may pass a decree in terms of the compromise, although it has jurisdiction limited to suits 
of which the value of the subject matter does not exceed Rs 5,000.14

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint for damages for defamation and permanent injunction
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE DISTRICT COURT AT {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}, {{STATE}}

PLAINT FILED BY:
Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.
Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

Suit for damages for defamation and permanent injunction under the Civil Procedure Code and law for the time being in force against the Defendant for false and malicious publication on Facebook.

Facts: The Plaintiff runs a business and suffers loss of goodwill and mental agony due to false and malicious statements published by the Defendant on Facebook, which publication was accessible to third parties and intended to disparage Plaintiff's business reputation. The wording of the defamatory statement is reproduced below:
{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENTS_TEXT}}

The statements published by the Defendant were false and malicious, and were published with knowledge of their falsity or with reckless disregard for the truth (malice) and were made with the intention to injure the Plaintiff's reputation. The publication was made to third parties, viz., the Plaintiff's customers and prospective business associates, resulting in loss of goodwill and mental agony to the Plaintiff.

Cause of action:
1. Date/event when underlying obligation arose: The defaming statements were published on or about {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}.
2. Accrual of right to sue: The Plaintiff's right to sue accrued on the date of first publication, i.e., {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}.
3. Continuing nature: The cause of action is of a continuing nature, as the Defendant continues to publish or permit publication of defamatory material on Facebook and other social media platforms.

Limitation: The suit is within the period of limitation for defamation; One year from the date of first publication (as per the standard Schedule for torts such as defamation). The present claim is based on continuing publication and is timely if filed within the prescribed period. (Cited provisions: rag-doc-1)

Valuation and court fee:
Plaintiff claims damages of Rs. 10,00,000 (Rupees ten lacs) for loss of goodwill and mental agony.
The court fee payable shall be computed per the Court Fee framework applicable to this suit; the exact amount is to be verified at the time of filing and is indicated as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

Relief sought:
(a) damages in the sum of Rs. 10,00,000 along with interest as may be directed by the Court;
(b) permanent injunction restraining the Defendant from publishing any defamatory or similarly worded statements on Facebook or any other social media platform, and from circulating or publishing any further defamatory material relating to Plaintiff;
(c) costs of the suit; and
(d) any other relief as this Court may deem fit and proper.

Verification:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, Plaintiff above named, do hereby solemnly verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to ... of the plaint are true to my knowledge and belief and that I have not suppressed any material facts.

Annexures: Annexure A – Copy of the Facebook post (defamatory statements). Annexure B – Any other supporting documents.

Place: {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}; Date: {{VERIFICATION_DATE}}.

List of Documents / Enclosures
Annexure A: Copy of Facebook post
Annexure B: {{OTHER_DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}

blocking_issues.issue: Cause of action accrual date missing / not anchored to the date the right to sue accrued; accrual date placeholder present but no actual date; affects limitation analysis
blocking_issues.fix: Identify and insert the correct accrual date from the facts (date of first publication) and anchor the limitation period to that date; if continuing publication is involved, explicitly state continuing cause of action and the dates of subsequent publications as applicable

blocking_issues.issue: Defamatory statements text not provided in the body; required to reproduce defamatory statements or attach as Annexure A
blocking_issues.fix: Insert the exact defamatory statements or attach them as Annexure A and reference the Annexure in the body

blocking_issues.issue: Verification date (and place) missing in verification clause
blocking_issues.fix: Insert the verification date and the district/place of filing in the Verification clause; ensure the verification paragraph is properly dated and signed

blocking_issues.issue: Court fee amount not computed; placeholder used for COURT_FEE_AMOUNT and no rate × value computation shown

unsupported_statements.statement: The statements published by the Defendant were false and malicious.

final_artifacts.text:
IN THE DISTRICT COURT AT {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}, {{STATE}}

PLAINTIFF: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.
DEFENDANT: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

Suit for damages for defamation and permanent injunction under the Civil Procedure Code and the law for the time being in force against the Defendant for false and malicious publication on Facebook.

Facts: The Plaintiff runs a business and suffers loss of goodwill and mental agony due to defamatory statements published by the Defendant on Facebook, which were accessible to third parties and intended to disparage Plaintiff's business reputation. The defamatory statements are reproduced in Annexure A (Copy of the Facebook post). The statements were false and malicious and were published with knowledge of their falsity or with reckless disregard for the truth, with the intention to injure Plaintiff's reputation. The publication was made to third parties, viz., Plaintiff's customers and prospective business associates, resulting in loss of goodwill and mental agony.

Cause of Action: 1) Accrual: The cause of action accrued on or about {{FIRST_PUBLICATION_DATE}}, i.e., the date of first publication of the defamatory statements. 2) Accrual of right to sue: The Plaintiff's right to sue accrues on the date of first publication and continues as long as the Defendant continues to publish or permit publication of defamatory material. 3) Continuing nature: The cause of action is of a continuing nature, as the Defendant continues to publish or permit publication of defamatory material on Facebook and other social media platforms.

Limitation: The suit is within the period of limitation for defamation; One year from the date of first publication, with continuing publication sustaining the claim within the statutory limit. (Note: For the purposes of the plaint, the continuing nature is asserted and will be refined upon filing based on dates of subsequent publications.) The present claim is based on continuing publication and is timely if filed within the prescribed period. (Refer to factual accrual and continuing action)

Valuation and court fee:
Plaintiff claims damages of Rs. 10,00,000 (Rupees ten lacs) for loss of goodwill and mental agony.
The court fee payable shall be computed per the Court Fee Schedule applicable to this suit; the exact amount will be verified at filing and is indicated as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

Relief sought:
(a) damages in the sum of Rs. 10,00,000 along with interest as may be directed by the Court;
(b) permanent injunction restraining the Defendant from publishing any defamatory or similarly worded statements on Facebook or any other social media platform, and from circulating or publishing any further defamatory material relating to Plaintiff;
(c) costs of the suit; and
(d) any other relief as this Court may deem fit and proper.

Verification:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to ... of the plaint are true to my knowledge and belief and that I have not suppressed any material facts.
Place: {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}; Date: {{VERIFICATION_DATE}}.

Annexures: Annexure A – Copy of Facebook post; Annexure B – {{OTHER_DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}.

Place: {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}; Date: {{VERIFICATION_DATE}}.
